text.title: PM Agent Workflow and Integration Patterns
text.summary: Subagent patterns for PM digest generation and risk scoring, prompt structures, CLI tools, and failure modes.

section.title: Agentic Architecture
section.p: A Claude subagent is well-suited to the data-aggregation and synthesis layer: pull structured data from project APIs, run heuristic risk scoring, generate stakeholder digests, surface anomalies. Never let agents trigger budget reallocations or deadline changes autonomously — gate all writes behind a human confirmation step.

rule.statement: Use Haiku for routine weekly digest generation; use Sonnet for retrospective pattern analysis across multiple sprints.
rule.rationale: Weekly digests are pattern application on structured JSON — low reasoning demand. Retrospective pattern analysis requires cross-sprint reasoning about causes and trends.

rule.statement: Scope AI automation to observation and synthesis; keep action triggers human-initiated.
rule.rationale: An agent that auto-closes risks or auto-updates status fields can corrupt the PM system state. The PM tool is the source of truth; agent writes without human approval destroy auditability.

section.title: Prompt Patterns
section.reference: Project status digest from sprint JSON data
Risk scoring script: Low/Medium/High per register item

section.title: CLI Tools
section.p: jira-cli: query Jira issues, sprints, boards from terminal. install: brew install ankitpokhrel/tap/jira-cli
gh (GitHub CLI): pull PR/issue data into agent context. install: brew install gh
gsheet-cli: read/write Google Sheets for budget tracking. github.com/lidaobing/gsheet-cli
mlflow CLI: track experiment runs if AI models are embedded in delivery pipeline. pip install mlflow

rule.statement: Log the record count fetched versus expected from Jira/Linear on every API call — rate limits can silently truncate data.
rule.rationale: Silent truncation gives a partial sprint view that looks complete. Risk scores and velocity calculations built on partial data produce false confidence.

rule.statement: Timestamp-gate sprint data queries: if the agent runs mid-sprint, partial data skews velocity calculations.
rule.rationale: Sprint boundary detection requires knowing whether the sprint has ended. Running on day 3 of 14 gives data that cannot be compared to completed-sprint baselines.

antipattern.description: LLM hallucinating task IDs and dates in long-context report generation.
antipattern.reason: Always validate task IDs and dates against the source PM system before including in reports; hallucinated IDs cause stakeholder confusion and erode trust.

antipattern.description: AI-generated summaries reporting "green" status that hides a critical qualitative flag.
antipattern.reason: AI risk scoring is only as good as its structured inputs; qualitative concerns from team members (unstated in tickets) don't appear in data-driven summaries.

antipattern.description: Pinning to no model version for digest agents.
antipattern.reason: A Haiku upgrade can change summary tone noticeably — pin the model; digest style should be consistent across reports.
